data.content: The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group. Globs expand at **evaluation time** (before targets run), so the file is invisible to the compiler. The fix is to explicitly add it to `Compile` and `FileWrites` in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file during the build, but it doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing happens at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up by the compiler.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, after writing it. I also need to add it to `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the `including-generated-files` skill since it's specifically about this exact problem.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>

      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's what was wrong and the fix:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` during build execution, but MSBuild's implicit file globbing runs at **evaluation time** (before any targets execute). So the generated file exists on disk but is never passed to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly include the generated file in:
- `<Compile>` — so Roslyn compiles it
- `<FileWrites>` — so `dotnet clean` removes it
